problem_statement: An educational media packager began publishing playable lessons whose captions stop immediately after an authoring note. The source tracks still validate, packaging reports success, and the onset follows a routine builder refresh. Determine the root cause using retained artifacts and read-only comparisons; production mutation is not required.

observations.statement: Across eight affected lessons, the player loads every cue before the first NOTE block and zero cues after it, while video and audio segment counts remain normal.
observations.provenance: player telemetry export joined to packaged cue indexes

observations.statement: The retained WebVTT inputs have the same SHA-256 values as the authoring exports and pass two independent syntax validators, including the blank line following each NOTE block.
observations.provenance: immutable authoring export and validation reports

observations.statement: Package manifests claim the expected total cue count, but the serialized seek index contains only the prefix ending at the first note; the loss therefore occurs after counting and before index serialization.
observations.provenance: signed package manifest and extracted seek index

observations.statement: Application source, packager settings, locale, and container base digest are identical between the final good build and the first bad build.
observations.provenance: build attestations from adjacent nightly runs

observations.statement: The resolved component lock differs at one transitive caption parser: the good build resolves 2.8.4 and the bad build resolves 3.0.0 after the lock was regenerated.
A quarantined offline comparison feeds identical retained bytes to both resolved component sets: 2.8.4 emits 231 cues, while 3.0.0 emits 74 and leaves its scanner in note mode at end of input.
Tracks with no NOTE blocks produce byte-identical indexes under both component sets, ruling against a general serializer, storage, or player regression.
The archived 3.0.0 parser source changes NOTE termination from one blank-line token to a two-token delimiter that the tokenizer can never emit as a pair.
observations.provenance: vendor source archive matched to the resolved digest

observations.statement: Rebuilding an affected package in isolation with the prior resolved component restores the full seek index without changing application code, input bytes, settings, or runtime image.
observations.provenance: sealed restoration build and artifact diff
